  • Method
Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotImage

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotElement
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotImage
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPotElement
public class PotImage extends PotElement
Represents an image element within a PowerPoint slide.

This class provides a fluent API for manipulating image properties such as position, size, cropping, borders, and effects. It wraps the underlying Apache POI XSLFPictureShape and XSLFPictureData objects, offering higher-level operations while maintaining compatibility with the POI model. Images can be replaced, exported, duplicated, and styled with borders and various visual effects.

Basic Usage Example


 PotImage image = slide.addImage("photo.jpg")
     .at(100, 100)
     .size(400, 300)
     .border(PotBorder.of(PotColor.GRAY, 1));

Cropping Examples


 image.crop(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1);  // Crop 10% from each side
 image.cropToShape(PotShapeType.OVAL);  // Crop to an oval shape (if supported)
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Method Details

    • getImageData

      public byte[] getImageData()
      Retrieves the raw byte data of the embedded image.
      Returns:
      the image byte array, or null if no picture data is associated

    • getPictureType

      public org.apache.poi.sl.usermodel.PictureData.PictureType getPictureType()
      Retrieves the picture type of the embedded image.
      Returns:
      the picture type (e.g., PNG, JPEG), or null if no picture data is associated

    • getContentType

      public String getContentType()
      Retrieves the MIME content type of the embedded image.
      Returns:
      the content type string (e.g., "image/png"), or null if no picture data is associated

    • getOriginalWidth

      public int getOriginalWidth()
      Retrieves the original width of the embedded image in pixels.
      Returns:
      the original width in pixels, or 0 if the dimension is unknown or unavailable

    • getOriginalHeight

      public int getOriginalHeight()
      Retrieves the original height of the embedded image in pixels.
      Returns:
      the original height in pixels, or 0 if the dimension is unknown or unavailable

    • getFileName

      public String getFileName()
      Retrieves the original file name of the embedded image.
      Returns:
      the file name, or null if no picture data is associated

    • replace

      public PotImage replace(String imagePath)
      Replaces the current image with the image from the specified file path.

      The image file is read from disk, and its type is inferred from the file extension. The underlying POI picture data is updated, and the shape's XML is modified accordingly.

      Parameters:
      imagePath - the file system path to the new image file
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      PotException - if an I/O error occurs while reading the file
      See Also:
      • replace(File)
      • replace(byte[], PictureData.PictureType)

    • replace

      public PotImage replace(File file)
      Replaces the current image with the image from the specified file.

      The image file is read from disk, and its type is inferred from the file name. The underlying POI picture data is updated, and the shape's XML is modified accordingly.

      Parameters:
      file - the new image file
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      PotException - if an I/O error occurs while reading the file
      See Also:
      • replace(byte[], PictureData.PictureType)

    • replace

      public PotImage replace(byte[] imageData, String contentType)
      Replaces the current image with the provided image data and content type.

      The picture type is inferred from the content type string (e.g., "image/jpeg"). The underlying POI picture data is updated, and the shape's XML is modified accordingly.

      Parameters:
      imageData - the raw byte data of the new image
      contentType - the MIME content type of the image (e.g., "image/jpeg")
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • replace(byte[], PictureData.PictureType)

    • replace

      public PotImage replace(byte[] imageData, org.apache.poi.sl.usermodel.PictureData.PictureType pictureType)
      Replaces the current image with the provided image data and picture type.

      This method adds the new picture data to the presentation and updates the shape's XML reference to point to the new data. The previous image data is not automatically removed from the presentation file.

      Parameters:
      imageData - the raw byte data of the new image
      pictureType - the picture type of the new image
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining

    • crop

      public PotImage crop(double top, double right, double bottom, double left)
      Applies a crop to the image by specifying fractional offsets from each side.

      The crop values are fractions between 0.0 and 1.0, representing the proportion of the image to remove from each side. For example, a top value of 0.1 crops away 10% of the image from the top. The method modifies the OpenXML srcRect element within the shape's XML.

      Values are clamped to the valid range [0.0, 1.0] and converted to integer percentages (0-100000) as required by the OpenXML specification.

      Parameters:
      top - the fraction to crop from the top (0.0-1.0)
      right - the fraction to crop from the right (0.0-1.0)
      bottom - the fraction to crop from the bottom (0.0-1.0)
      left - the fraction to crop from the left (0.0-1.0)
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining

    • getClipping

      public Insets getClipping()
      Retrieves the current clipping (crop) insets of the image.

      This method delegates to the underlying POI XSLFPictureShape.getClipping(). The returned insets represent the cropped portions from each side in pixels.

      Returns:
      the current clipping insets, or null if not available

    • removeCrop

      public PotImage removeCrop()
      Removes any applied crop from the image.

      Note: This implementation currently returns without modifying the image, as POI may not provide a direct method to remove clipping. Future versions may implement proper crop removal.

      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining

    • cropToAspect

      public PotImage cropToAspect(double aspectWidth, double aspectHeight)
      Crops the image to a specific aspect ratio.

      The image is cropped equally from the sides or top/bottom to achieve the target aspect ratio while preserving the original image's center. If the image already matches the target ratio, no crop is applied.

      Usage Example

      
 // Crop to a 16:9 widescreen aspect ratio
 image.cropToAspect(16, 9);
      Parameters:
      aspectWidth - the width component of the target aspect ratio (e.g., 16)
      aspectHeight - the height component of the target aspect ratio (e.g., 9)
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • cropToSquare()

    • cropToSquare

      public PotImage cropToSquare()
      Crops the image to a square aspect ratio (1:1).

      This is a convenience method that delegates to cropToAspect(double, double) with both parameters set to 1.

      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • cropToAspect(double, double)

    • border

      public PotImage border(PotBorder border)
      Applies a border to the image.

      The border properties (color, width, dash style, and cap style) are taken from the provided PotBorder object. If the border is null, the method calls noBorder() to remove any existing border.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.of(PotColor.RED, 2.0)
     .dash(PotDashStyle.DASH)
     .cap(PotCapStyle.ROUND);
 image.border(border);
      Parameters:
      border - the border configuration to apply
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • borderColor(PotColor)
      • borderWidth(double)
      • noBorder()

    • borderColor

      public PotImage borderColor(PotColor color)
      Sets the border color of the image.

      This method only updates the border color; other border properties remain unchanged. If the image currently has no border (zero width), setting a color will not make the border visible until a width is also set.

      Parameters:
      color - the new border color
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • borderWidth(double)
      • border(PotBorder)

    • borderWidth

      public PotImage borderWidth(double width)
      Sets the border width of the image.

      The width is specified in points. A width of zero effectively removes the border. If the border color has not been set, the default color (usually black) is used.

      Parameters:
      width - the new border width in points
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • borderColor(PotColor)
      • border(PotBorder)

    • noBorder

      public PotImage noBorder()
      Removes the border from the image.

      This is achieved by setting the border width to zero. The border color and other style properties are preserved but will not be visible.

      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • border(PotBorder)

    • resetToOriginalSize

      public PotImage resetToOriginalSize()
      Resets the image to its original pixel dimensions.

      The image is resized to match the dimensions of the embedded image data as reported by getOriginalWidth() and getOriginalHeight(). If the original dimensions are unavailable, no change is made.

      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • scaleToWidth(double)
      • scaleToHeight(double)

    • scaleToWidth

      public PotImage scaleToWidth(double width)
      Scales the image proportionally to a specific width.

      The height is adjusted automatically to maintain the original aspect ratio. If the original dimensions are unavailable, no change is made.

      Parameters:
      width - the target width in points
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • scaleToHeight(double)
      • scaleToFit(double, double)

    • scaleToHeight

      public PotImage scaleToHeight(double height)
      Scales the image proportionally to a specific height.

      The width is adjusted automatically to maintain the original aspect ratio. If the original dimensions are unavailable, no change is made.

      Parameters:
      height - the target height in points
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • scaleToWidth(double)
      • scaleToFit(double, double)

    • scaleToFit

      public PotImage scaleToFit(double maxWidth, double maxHeight)
      Scales the image proportionally to fit within a bounding box.

      The image is scaled down (if necessary) so that both its width and height are less than or equal to the specified maximum dimensions, while preserving the original aspect ratio. If the image is already smaller, it is not enlarged.

      Usage Example

      
 // Ensure the image fits within a 400x300 area
 image.scaleToFit(400, 300);
      Parameters:
      maxWidth - the maximum allowed width in points
      maxHeight - the maximum allowed height in points
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • scaleToFill(double, double)
      • scaleToWidth(double)

    • scaleToFill

      public PotImage scaleToFill(double targetWidth, double targetHeight)
      Scales the image proportionally to completely fill a bounding box.

      The image is scaled up (if necessary) so that both its width and height are at least the specified target dimensions, while preserving the original aspect ratio. This may cause the image to extend beyond the target box, which is typically used in conjunction with cropping.

      Usage Example

      
 // Scale the image to cover a 400x300 area (may overflow)
 image.scaleToFill(400, 300);
      Parameters:
      targetWidth - the minimum target width in points
      targetHeight - the minimum target height in points
      Returns:
      this PotImage instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • scaleToFit(double, double)
      • cropToAspect(double, double)

    • exportTo

      public void exportTo(File file)
      Exports the embedded image data to a file.

      The raw image bytes are written directly to the specified file. The file format is determined by the embedded image type (e.g., PNG, JPEG). If no image data is available, the method does nothing.

      Parameters:
      file - the destination file
      Throws:
      PotException - if an I/O error occurs while writing the file
      See Also:
      • exportTo(OutputStream)

    • exportTo

      public void exportTo(OutputStream outputStream)
      Exports the embedded image data to an output stream.

      The raw image bytes are written directly to the provided stream. The stream is not closed by this method. If no image data is available, the method does nothing.

      Parameters:
      outputStream - the destination output stream
      Throws:
      PotException - if an I/O error occurs while writing to the stream
      See Also:
      • exportTo(File)

    • duplicate

      public PotImage duplicate()
      Creates a duplicate of this image on the same slide.

      The duplicate is placed slightly offset from the original (as defined by PotConstants.DUPLICATE_OFFSET_POINTS) and inherits the original's position, size, rotation, and cropping. The image data is reembedded into the presentation.

      If the original image has no embedded data, an UnsupportedOperationException is thrown.

      Specified by:
      duplicate in interface IPotElement
      Overrides:
      duplicate in class PotElement
      Returns:
      the newly created PotImage instance
      Throws:
      PotException - if the image cannot be duplicated due to missing data
      See Also:
      • PotSlide.addImage(byte[], String)

    • getRawPictureShape

      public org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XSLFPictureShape getRawPictureShape()
      Returns the underlying Apache POI XSLFPictureShape object.

      This method provides direct access to the POI shape for advanced operations not covered by the PotImage API. Modifications made directly to the returned object may affect the behavior of this PotImage instance.

      Returns:
      the wrapped POI picture shape

    • getRawPictureData

      public org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XSLFPictureData getRawPictureData()
      Returns the underlying Apache POI XSLFPictureData object.

      This method provides direct access to the POI picture data for advanced operations such as inspecting image metadata or performing lowlevel replacements.

      Returns:
      the underlying POI picture data, or null if not available
      See Also:
      • getRawPictureShape()

      Scales the element uniformly by the specified factor.

      Parameters:
      scale - the scaling factor; 1.0 represents 100%, must be positive
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Scales the element independently along the X and Y axes.

      Parameters:
      scaleX - the horizontal scaling factor; must be positive
      scaleY - the vertical scaling factor; must be positive
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotate

      public PotImage rotate(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotation

      public PotImage rotation(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element (fluent builder alias).
      Overrides:
      rotation in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotateBy

      public PotImage rotateBy(double deltaAngle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Rotates the element by the specified angle delta.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotateBy in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      deltaAngle - the angle to add to the current rotation, in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • flipHorizontal

      public PotImage flipHorizontal()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Toggles the horizontal flip state of the element.

      Flipping is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      flipHorizontal in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • flipVertical

      public PotImage flipVertical()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Toggles the vertical flip state of the element.

      Flipping is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      flipVertical in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringToFront

      public PotImage bringToFront()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element to the front of the slide's drawing order.

      This operation manipulates the underlying XML structure of the slide.

      Overrides:
      bringToFront in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendToBack

      public PotImage sendToBack()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element to the back of the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendToBack in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringForward

      public PotImage bringForward()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element one step forward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      bringForward in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendBackward

      public PotImage sendBackward()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element one step backward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendBackward in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • animate

      public PotImage animate(PotAnimation animation)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies an animation to this element.
      Overrides:
      animate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      animation - the animation to apply; must not be null
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeAnimation

      public PotImage removeAnimation()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any animation attached to this element.
      Overrides:
      removeAnimation in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotImage hyperlink(String url)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink to this element that points to an external URL.

      Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      url - the destination URL; must not be null
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotImage hyperlink(PotHyperlink link)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink defined by a PotHyperlink object.

      If the provided link is null, any existing hyperlink is removed. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      link - the hyperlink configuration, or null to remove the hyperlink
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlinkToSlide

      public PotImage hyperlinkToSlide(int slideIndex)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Creates a hyperlink that jumps to a specific slide within the same presentation.

      This method sets both the internal slide reference and a fallback address string. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlinkToSlide in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      slideIndex - the zerobased index of the target slide
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeHyperlink

      public PotImage removeHyperlink()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any hyperlink attached to this element.

      This method manipulates the underlying XML because Apache POI does not provide a direct removal API. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      removeHyperlink in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • action

      public PotImage action(PotAction action)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches an action (e.g., mouseclick or mouseover) to the element.
      Overrides:
      action in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      action - the action configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeAction

      public PotImage removeAction(boolean isHover)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes an action from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeAction in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      isHover - true to remove a mouseover action, false to remove a mouseclick action
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • shadow

      public PotImage shadow(PotShadow shadow)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a shadow effect to the element.

      Shadows are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected. Passing null or a shadow of type PotShadow.Type.NONE removes any existing shadow.

      Overrides:
      shadow in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      shadow - the shadow configuration, or null to remove the shadow
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeShadow

      public PotImage removeShadow()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any shadow effect from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeShadow in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      public PotImage reflection(PotEffect.Reflection reflection)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a reflection effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      reflection in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      reflection - the reflection configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Applies a glow effect to the element.
      Overrides:

      Parameters:
      glow - the glow configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Applies a softedge effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      softEdge in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      softEdge - the softedge configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeEffects

      public PotImage removeEffects()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes all visual effects (shadow, reflection, glow, softedge, 3D) from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeEffects in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Overrides:
      rotation3D in class PotElement
      Parameters:

      Returns:

      Parameters:

      Parameters:

    • lighting

      public PotImage lighting(Pot3DFormat.Lighting lighting)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a lighting effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      lighting in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      lighting - the lighting configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • opacity

      public PotImage opacity(double opacity)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the opacity (transparency) of the element.

      Opacity is applied by manipulating the underlying XML, as Apache POI does not provide a direct API. The value must be between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).

      Overrides:
      opacity in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      opacity - the opacity value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque)
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class PotElement
